name: Ten Thousand Tsumego
why: The repository documents a large tsumego app and exposes useful training categories, but its problem folders reference classic book collections. GoMentor should not import raw positions from those books without a separate rights review.

name: Joseki-Master
why: The repository is MIT licensed and its collection names give a compact joseki training taxonomy. GoMentor uses original teaching summaries rather than copying collection data verbatim.

name: Kogo's Joseki Dictionary Chinese SGF
why: The repository contains a translated SGF but no explicit license. It is useful only as a reminder that joseki content needs careful licensing.

name: OGS godojo-server
why: The README identifies it as a server for OGS joseki features, but no license was found during review.

name: tsumego-hero refactor
why: The repository is a modernized tsumego-hero codebase. It is useful for product and data-flow ideas, not for importing problem data.

name: Josekipedia game collection
license: Creative Commons stated for game database
why: Josekipedia states that its game database is freely distributed under a Creative Commons License and discards comments/variations. This may be useful for future joseki frequency mining, but GoMentor currently only records it as a potential source until the exact CC terms and attribution path are reviewed.

name: Wikipedia: Life and death
why: The page summarizes fundamental life-and-death concepts such as two eyes, dead groups, unsettled groups, and eye-space. GoMentor uses original teaching text and does not copy article prose.

name: Wikipedia: Go game rules and strategy overview
why: The article summarizes life status, semeai, corners/sides opening priority, joseki, ko, and seki. GoMentor uses it only to cross-check broad coverage.

name: goproblems.com best practices
why: The page describes common problem classes such as life-and-death and tesuji. It is useful as a taxonomy reference, but GoMentor does not import problem text or diagrams.

name: Online Go Forum: Go books/content in the Creative Commons
license: forum content, not a data source
why: The discussion highlights that Internet availability and archive copies do not imply Creative Commons rights. GoMentor uses this as a policy reminder to avoid importing classic book scans or problem collections without explicit permission.

name: GoMentor original common-pattern training catalog
why: The catalog contains original teaching reconstructions of common joseki, life-and-death, and tesuji patterns. It deliberately avoids copying book diagrams, problem ordering, SGF comments, or website explanations.

name: KataGo Analysis Engine documentation
license: KataGo project license; documentation used as API reference
why: Primary source for KataGo analysis JSON fields.

name: KataGo project README
why: Primary source for KataGo capabilities such as score/territory estimation and JSON analysis engine.

name: Wikibooks: Go
license: Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike License unless otherwise noted by Wikibooks
why: Wikibooks has Go lessons including joseki and strategy topics.

name: Kogo's Joseki Dictionary
license: Free download stated by publisher page; redistribution and derivative-use terms are not explicitly clear in the public page
why: Public page describes KJD as an extensive SGF joseki dictionary and says it was last updated in 2014.

name: EidoGo Kogo joseki viewer
license: Site/source terms should be reviewed before reuse
why: Useful for human verification of joseki direction, not a bundled data source.

name: Pachi joseki module README
why: Describes radius-based local pattern matching, ignored/bad moves, and simple joseki conventions.

name: Pachi josekifix README
why: Describes matching by local pattern around last move and optional external joseki engine.

name: Josekle project
license: MIT for project code; upstream OGS joseki data access and commentary terms should be reviewed separately
why: Useful for UX and quiz design, not as authoritative joseki truth.

name: GoGoD database
why: Commercial game database; useful for future paid data QA, not included in GoMentor data.

name: GoMentor curated original teaching cards
why: Cards are not copied from books or joseki dictionaries. They encode common teaching patterns and should be continuously reviewed by human coaches.

name: Pachi joseki SGF set
why: Bundled as third-party GPL joseki SGF data with COPYING preserved. Teacher output treats matches as hypotheses and still defers final recommendations to KataGo evidence.

name: Josekle dictionary SGF
why: Bundled as third-party MIT joseki SGF data with LICENSE preserved. Used for SGF-derived prefix cards and joseki-tree hypotheses.
